sections.title: SCOPE
sections.text: This Standard applies to oat grains as defined in Section 2 intended for processing for direct human consumption. This Standard does not apply to Avena nuda (hulless oats).

sections.title: Toxic or noxious seeds
sections.text:
The products covered by the provisions of this Standard shall be free from the following toxic or noxious seeds in amounts which may represent a hazard to human health.
– Crotalaria (Crotalaria spp.), Corn cockle (Agrostemma githago L.), Castor bean. (Ricinus communis L.), Jimson weed (Datura spp.), and other seeds that are commonly recognized as harmful to health.

sections.title: Labelling of non-retail containers
sections.text: Information for non -retail containers shall either be given on the container or in accompanying documents, except that the name of the product, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer shall appear on the container. However, lot identification and the name and address of the manufacturer or packer may be replaced by an identification mark, provided that such a mark is clearly identifiable with the accompanying documents.

sections.title: ANNEX
sections.text:
In those instances where more than one factor limit and/or method of analysis is given it is strongly recommended that users specify the appropriate limit and method of analysis.

Factor/Description Limit Method of analysis
1 Minimum test weight:
The weight of a hundred litre volume of oats expressed as kilograms per hectolitre.
At least 46 kg/hl The test weight shall be the weight per ISO 7971 -1986 or any other equipment giving equivalent results expressed as kilograms per hectolitre as determined on a test portion of the original sample
2 Hull-less and broken kernels (kernels with no hulls and broken of any size).
5% m/m max To be developed
3 Edible grains other than oats (whole or identifiably broken).
3% m/m max To be developed
4 Damaged kernels (including pieces of kernels that show visible deterioration due to moisture, weather, disease, insects, mould, heating, fermentation, sprouting or other causes).
3% m/m max To be developed
5 Wild oats: Aventa fatua or Aventa sterilis.
0.2% m/m max To be developed
6 Insect bored kernels: kernels which have been visibly bored or tunnelled by insects.
0.5% m/m max To be developed
7 Blemished grains, i.e. grains with stained hulls due to the action of climatic factors.
To be decided To be developed

classification.sentence: In addition to the requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985), the following specific provisions apply:
classification.pattern: [Referential clause indicating baseline standard], [deictic phrase indicating added provisions], [main clause stating applicability of these provisions]

components.baseline_standard: requirements of the General Standard for the Labelling of Prepackaged Foods (CXS 1-1985)

classification.justification: The sentence imposes an obligation by stating that additional specific provisions apply beyond an established baseline standard. The structure is a referential introduction combined with a deictic phrase and a main clause of obligation. No known template matches this exact structural pattern, resulting in a new pattern classification.
notes: The sentence functions as a normative extension clause referencing another standard and adding obligations, which is distinct from typical obligation templates focused on direct action requirements.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR OATS
pages.summary: Página inicial contendo título do documento 'STANDARD FOR OATS' e cabeçalho 'CXS 201-1995'. Sem seções ou texto hierarquizado nesta página.

pages.title: STANDARD FOR OATS
pages.summary: P�gina 2 do documento 'STANDARD FOR OATS' com o cabe�alho 'CXS 201-1995 2'. Estrutura hier�rquica composta por 5 se��es principais numeradas (1 a 5) incluindo subse���es, com textos associados pontuais. A se�����o 5 tem cont��nuas que se extendem para a pr��xima p��gina.

Página 3 do documento 'STANDARD FOR OATS', com cabeçalho 'CXS 201-1995 3'. Estrutura hierárquica apresenta continuação da seção 5 da página anterior, subdividida no item 5.3 com texto associado. Novas seções principais 6 (PACKAGING), 7 (LABELLING) e 8 (METHODS OF ANALYSIS AND SAMPLING) estão presentes, com subseções para 6 e 7, e textos adequados a cada nó. Continuidade da seção 5 devidamente marcada. Estrutura clara para uso em RAG.

Página apresenta um título principal não numerado 'ANNEX' no nível 1 com texto explicativo e tabela detalhada associada. Não há outras subdivisões hierárquicas evidentes na página. Cabeçalho identificado como 'CXS 201-1995 4'.
